title: Receipt polling ignores the chain used for the transaction
reasoning: The write uses the wallet's currentChainId and the factory address for that chain, but receipt polling always uses blockdagMainnet and DEFAULT_CHAIN_ID. If currentChainId is any supported non-default chain, the transaction can be submitted successfully while the UI waits on the wrong RPC, times out, and sends the user to the dashboard instead of the new escrow.
recommendation: Create the public client from currentChainId using the same chain/RPC selection as the write path, or enforce/switch the wallet to the single supported default chain before allowing submission.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests cover receipt polling with a non-default chain id or assert that the polling RPC matches the transaction chain.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock useChainId to a non-default supported chain, submit an escrow, and assert createPublicClient/http are called with the RPC for that same chain id.
minimumFixScope: Update receipt polling client construction in handleSubmit and add a chain mismatch guard if only one chain is intended.
